finding_used: Competing outcomes should be modeled with calibrated event probabilities under proper scoring rules.
limitation: Round 50 has no censoring because timeout is an observed terminal class, so it uses ordinary joint multinomial log loss rather than importing the paper's censoring adjustment.

finding_used: Time-to-event forecasts require scoring rules that target the predictive distribution rather than an arbitrary concordance surrogate.
limitation: Medical survival results are not financial-edge evidence.

finding_used: Log likelihood is a proper distributional objective, while common ranking-style survival metrics need not be proper.
limitation: Round 50 measures rank only as an out-of-sample diagnostic and does not train on a concordance objective.

finding_used: Distributional target representations can stabilize noisy regression, motivating a future categorical payoff ablation if the simpler event decomposition is insufficient.
limitation: Round 50 does not claim that classification universally dominates regression.

finding_used: Categorical objectives can be more stable than scalar regression in noisy, non-stationary value estimation.
limitation: The study is reinforcement learning; Round 50 is supervised and keeps direct mean regression as the matched control.

finding_used: Forecast accuracy alone need not produce actionable trading performance, requiring a separate executable replay with costs.
limitation: The paper's limit-order-book setting and reported values are not imported.

finding_used: Walk-forward crypto evidence reinforces cost-aware abstention and evaluation after realistic friction.
limitation: It is a recent preprint and cannot establish Round 50 profitability.

finding_used: Independent deep seeds provide a practical disagreement signal for selective abstention.
limitation: Disagreement is not a calibrated loss bound.
One-minute source resolution; no multi-year second-level claim.
Safety reasoning only; financial edge was not tested.
